name: status_narrative_mismatch_na_with_affirming
description:
Pins the OPPOSITE direction of the STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH cross-status branch at engine/validator.py:481-490 already pinned by status_narrative_mismatch_cross_status.json. That sibling case covers GAP_DESCRIBING-narrative + COMPLIANT-status (LLM writes gap language but marks Compliant). This case covers COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING-narrative + NOT_APPLICABLE-status -- the LLM writes assessment-act language proving the control IS implemented locally, then mislabels the verdict as Not Applicable. Realistic substrate: a program team that inherits physical security from a tenant building MIGHT mark PE-3.1 as NA, but the LLM retrieves a local Physical Security Plan describing badge readers and reflexively writes affirming prose -- a 3PAO red flag because the verdict and the narrative contradict each other.

The cross-status branch (validator.py:481-490) is symmetric -- `if status is not None and status != expected_status` -- so a regression in either direction would silently flip one branch while leaving the other passing. Without this case, breaking only the COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING-vs-NA direction would let the sibling case still pass and a misclassified NA verdict could ship.

Mechanism trace:
  - status = NOT_APPLICABLE.
  - Narrative contains three _AFFIRMING_PHRASES (validator.py:43-60): 'Examined ' (line 57), 'confirmed via' (line 44), 'documented in' (line 54).
  - Narrative contains zero _NA_PHRASES (validator.py:63-80): no 'not applicable because', no 'system does not have', no 'implemented by aws/azure/gcp/csp', no 'outside system boundary', etc. The narrative DESCRIBES local implementation, the opposite of NA-justifying.
  - Narrative contains zero _GAP_PHRASES (validator.py:93-113): no 'no documentation', no 'poa&m', no 'remediation', no 'missing', no 'has not been'.
  - classes_hit = 1 (affirming only), so classify_narrative returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING unambiguously (not AMBIGUOUS, which would route to the OTHER branch at validator.py:471-480).
  - _expected_status_for_class(COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING) returns COMPLIANT (validator.py:736-737).
  - status (NOT_APPLICABLE) != expected_status (COMPLIANT) -> cross-status branch fires at validator.py:481-490.
  - Three identical proposals exhaust the retry budget -> _abstain with reason 'validator-exhausted: ... status_narrative_mismatch ...'.
  - Per Fix 3 (KERNEL_VERSION 0.5.0): hard-abstain coerces status to None and preserves the LLM's intended verdict on proposed_status. expected.proposed_status='Not Applicable' guards that coercion path for the NA direction (sibling case pins it for the COMPLIANT direction).

Load-bearing details that ISOLATE the cross-status branch from cascades:
  - Narrative does NOT match _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES (validator.py:117-122). Opens with 'Examined USD00088888' -- none of the four restatement openers fire.
  - Narrative token-set Jaccard vs procedures stays well below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70. Procedures: 'Examine documented physical access authorizations and entry/exit point listings.' Narrative tokens (after _TOKEN_RE filter at validator.py:779 [a-z0-9]{3,}): examined, usd00088888, example-system, physical, security, plan, rev, confirmed, via, section, badge-reader, access, controls, documented, plan, example-system, sterling, heights, facility, entry, exit, points -- ~20 distinct content tokens. Procedures tokens: examine, documented, physical, access, authorizations, and, entry, exit, point, listings -- ~10 distinct. Intersection: documented, physical, access, entry, exit -- 5 tokens. Jaccard = 5/20 = 0.25, well below 0.70. _is_requirement_restatement returns False -> REQUIREMENT_RESTATEMENT does NOT cascade.
  - Same Jaccard check passes for guidance (different vocabulary: 'enforces', 'authorizations', 'facility', 'resides') -- no cascade.
  - Narrative does NOT match _FUTURE_TENSE_RE (validator.py:187-198). All verbs present-tense ('are documented', 'badge-reader access controls'). Future-tense gate at validator.py:541-555 requires status=COMPLIANT anyway, so it would skip on NA status, but belt-and-suspenders.
  - No 'inherited from' string -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER skipped (validator.py:508-516).
  - No SV-#####r#_rule citation -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped (validator.py:570-587); also requires status=COMPLIANT to fire, which doesn't apply here.
  - USD00088888 IS in tagged_evidence so cite-verifier (validator.py:_verify_cites) passes -- the only USD token in the narrative is USD00088888 and it appears literally in evidence_text. UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION does NOT cascade.
  - No hybrid-split markers (no 'On-prem:', 'Cloud:', '## responsibility_split'). _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE at validator.py:140-142 does not match, so the hybrid-merge path is skipped and classify_narrative runs the single-narrative path that gives the clean COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING classification we need.

Three identical proposals (not three variants): the stub does not consume corrective_context, so the orchestrator's job is just to keep retrying until the budget is gone. Same retry-exhaustion contract pinned by status_narrative_mismatch_cross_status / future_tense_rejection / unsupported_doc_citation / uncorroborated_stig_pass_rejection.

Control PE-3.1 (Physical Access Control), CCI-000919 is a realistic substrate: many CONUS programs DO inherit physical from a tenant building (legitimate NA via lease + parent-system inheritance), but the LLM retrieving a local Physical Security Plan that describes facility-specific badge readers and reflexively writing 'Examined ... confirmed via ... documented in ...' affirming prose is exactly the failure mode the cross-status branch was written to catch.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization enforces physical access authorizations at entry and exit points to the facility where the information system resides.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented physical access authorizations and entry/exit point listings.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00088888 Example System Physical Security Plan Rev D — describes badge-reader access control at the Example System Sterling Heights facility, including the designated entry and exit points and the roster of authorized personnel.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined USD00088888 Example System Physical Security Plan Rev D; confirmed via section 3 that badge-reader access controls are documented in the plan for the Example System Sterling Heights facility entry and exit points.

Examined USD00088888 Example System Physical Security Plan Rev D; confirmed via section 3 that badge-reader access controls are documented in the plan for the Example System Sterling Heights facility entry and exit points.
